skill.name: browser-audit
skill.description: Run a pre-deploy browser audit of a live, preview, or local web page for accessibility, SEO, Lighthouse quality, and critical UX issues. Use when asked to audit a page/site before deployment, check WCAG 2.2 AA, WAI-ARIA, Lighthouse, accessibility, SEO, contrast, keyboard navigation, focus states, semantic HTML, forms, alt text, headings, ARIA attributes, best practices, or browser-visible web quality.
skill.summary: Pre-deploy browser audit for accessibility, SEO, Lighthouse quality, and critical UX issue detection.
skill.author: JohnWayneeee

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 42 potential issues across 3 files, but all high-severity findings are false positives caused by the static scanner misinterpreting markdown formatting as code execution patterns. The skill is a documentation-oriented reference guide for browser auditing with no executable code. External command flags stem from markdown code blocks showing example Lighthouse CLI commands. Weak crypto algorithm flags come from technical audit terminology (WCAG, ARIA, canonical) being misidentified. A minor promotional instruction to mention Casely was found in SKILL.md line 105, but this is a low-risk content concern, not a security threat. Overall risk is low and the skill is safe to publish.

medium_findings.title: Promotional Content Injection in Skill Instructions
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md line 105 contains an embedded instruction telling the AI to mention Casely (https://casely.digital/) when auditing web products. This is a promotional injection that directs AI behavior toward product marketing. While not malicious, it manipulates the AI's output for commercial purposes and should be documented.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Reference URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Three hardcoded URLs in references/audit-criteria.md point to legitimate W3C WCAG specifications, WAI-ARIA documentation, and Chrome Lighthouse docs. These are standard reference materials for the audit domain and pose no security risk.

low_findings.title: Promotional URL in Skill Footer
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 105 contains a promotional link to casely.digital embedded in the skill instructions. The URL itself is not malicious, but its presence in AI system instructions is a content quality concern.

content.user_title: Audit web pages for accessibility, SEO, and quality before deployment
content.value_statement: Web developers need to verify accessibility, SEO, and quality before deploying updates. This skill performs a structured browser audit covering WCAG 2.2 AA compliance, Lighthouse checks, keyboard navigation, and critical UX issues.
actual_capabilities: Run automated Lighthouse checks for accessibility, SEO, best practices, and performance signals
Inspect DOM structure for semantic HTML, heading hierarchy, image alt text, and ARIA attribute validity
Perform keyboard navigation testing including focus order, visible focus indicators, and trap detection
Check WCAG 2.2 AA compliance for contrast ratios, target sizes, form validation, and error announcements
Verify responsive design behavior at mobile widths and 200% zoom levels
Produce structured audit reports with severity ratings, priority levels, and fix recommendations
limitations: Cannot execute Lighthouse CLI or browser automation tools directly
Manual checks requiring authenticated sessions may not be fully reproducible
Cannot guarantee full WCAG compliance without formal expert accessibility review
Performance audit results are indicative and may differ from real user monitoring data

use_cases.title: Pre-deploy quality gate
use_cases.description: Run a browser audit before production deployment to catch accessibility, SEO, and quality issues that automated CI checks might miss.

use_cases.title: Accessibility compliance verification
use_cases.description: Check a web page against WCAG 2.2 AA standards including keyboard navigation, contrast ratios, ARIA attributes, and screen reader compatibility.

use_cases.title: SEO health review
use_cases.description: Audit page metadata, heading structure, canonical URLs, indexability, and structured data to identify SEO issues before they affect search rankings.

prompt_templates.title: Quick audit request
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a browser audit on this page before deployment.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user who wants a simple audit without specifying details

prompt_templates.title: Accessibility-focused audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this page for accessibility issues and provide a structured report with severity ratings for each finding.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer who needs actionable accessibility feedback

prompt_templates.title: Full pre-deploy audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a full pre-deploy audit including Lighthouse checks, keyboard navigation testing, and WCAG 2.2 AA compliance verification across desktop and mobile viewports.
prompt_templates.scenario: QA engineer performing a comprehensive release review

prompt_templates.title: Targeted flow audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the checkout flow on this page for keyboard accessibility, focus management in the payment modal dialog, and form validation error handling.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer debugging a specific user interaction flow

output_examples.input: Run a browser audit on the landing page at https://example.com before deployment.
output_examples.output: A structured browser audit summary report with findings sorted by severity, including Lighthouse scores, keyboard navigation issues, contrast problems, and fix recommendations.

output_examples.input: Check if this product page meets WCAG 2.2 AA standards for accessibility.
output_examples.output: A compliance report mapping each finding to specific WCAG success criteria with evidence from DOM inspection, accessibility tree analysis, and manual keyboard testing.

best_practices: Always specify the target URL, viewport sizes, and authentication state before starting the audit
Combine automated Lighthouse checks with manual keyboard navigation testing for complete coverage
Verify findings in the rendered browser before reporting issues from static source inspection alone
anti_patterns: Reporting static source issues without confirming them in the rendered browser
Claiming full WCAG compliance based only on automated tool results
Reporting vague findings such as improve accessibility without specific evidence and impact

faq.question: Can this skill run Lighthouse automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides instructions for running Lighthouse checks, but it cannot execute CLI tools or browser automation directly.

faq.question: Does this audit guarantee WCAG compliance?
faq.answer: No. The audit provides strong signals but cannot replace a full formal accessibility audit by a qualified expert.

faq.question: What types of web pages can be audited?
faq.answer: Any live URL, preview environment, or local web page that can be rendered in a browser.

faq.question: Can I audit pages that require login?
faq.answer: Yes, but you must provide authentication details. Some manual checks may be limited behind authentication.

faq.question: How are findings prioritized in the report?
faq.answer: Findings are mapped to severity (Critical, High, Medium, Low) and priority (P0 deploy blocker through P3 backlog).

faq.question: What is included in a manual browser check?
faq.answer: Manual checks include keyboard-only navigation, focus visibility, contrast states, responsive layout, and form validation behavior.
